results.question: Rocket Lab's most recent 10-K or 20-F discloses launch revenue and launch count separately, allowing calculation of an implied revenue-per-launch figure for its Electron vehicle. SpaceX's S-1 discloses Space segment revenue and launch-related KPIs including booster launches and flight-proven booster launches but does not provide a per-launch revenue figure. Using disclosed Space segment revenue and any launch volume data in the S-1, construct an implied revenue-per-launch proxy for SpaceX. Compare this directionally to Rocket Lab's disclosed figure. What does the comparison reveal about the effect of vertical integration and vehicle reusability on per-launch economics, and what specific disclosure in the SpaceX S-1 — present for Rocket Lab but absent here — would be required to make this comparison precise rather than approximate?
